title: fix(common/redis): re-AUTH new pool connections with fresh Entra token
body:
## Description

Fixes [#3554](https://github.com/dapr/components-contrib/issues/3554), the long-standing report that Entra ID auth against Azure Managed Redis stops working after the initial token's TTL elapses.

When `useEntraID: true`, the redis component snapshotted the initial Entra access token into `redis.UniversalOptions.Password` at component-init time. Every new pool connection opened by go-redis afterwards replayed that static AUTH at connection establishment — but the token has a finite TTL (1–24h depending on credential type and audience). After expiry, any new connection (idle timeout, `maxConnAge`, network blip, demand spike, pool churn) fails AUTH with `WRONGPASS invalid username-password pair`.

[#3632](https://github.com/dapr/components-contrib/pull/3632) fixed the background AUTH ACL refresh goroutine being killed by init context cancellation, but only re-AUTHs *existing* connections; it never updated `Options.Password`, so new connections continued to fail with the original snapshot once it expired. This explains the multiple post-#3632 reports on #3554 from users on 1.15.9, 1.16.1, 1.17.x ("token IS being refreshed in logs but pool reconnects fail").

## What this PR does

Installs an `OnConnect` hook on every variant of the underlying go-redis client (v8/v9, plain/cluster/failover) that:

1. Fetches a fresh token from the cached `*azcore.TokenCredential`.
2. Runs `AUTH ACL <oid> <fresh-token>` on the newly-dialed connection.

`azcore` caches tokens internally until ~5 minutes before expiry, so per-connection `GetToken` calls are inexpensive in steady state.

The static `Password` snapshot is no longer set, so go-redis skips its built-in AUTH during connection init and lets `OnConnect` handle authentication. The OID — used as the Redis ACL username, per the [Microsoft Entra for AMR documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/redis/entra-for-authentication) — is parsed once from the initial token at component init and stored on `Settings`; `OnConnect` uses it for every subsequent AUTH.

`StartEntraIDTokenRefreshBackgroundRoutine` is unchanged. It remains the safety net for long-lived connections that survive across token-TTL boundaries without ever being pool-cycled.

## Issue reference

Please reference the issue this PR will close: #3554

## Checklist

- [x] Code compiles correctly (`go vet ./common/component/redis/...`, `go build ./common/component/redis/...`, `go build` against all 5 redis-using components: `state/redis`, `pubsub/redis`, `bindings/redis`, `lock/redis`, `configuration/redis`)
- [x] Existing tests pass (`go test ./common/component/redis/... -count=1` — `ok 1.554s`)
- [x] Created/updated tests (`common/component/redis/entraid_onconnect_test.go` asserts `OnConnect` is wired and `Password` is unset when `UseEntraID` is true, and not wired when false, across all v8/v9 node/cluster/failover variants; plus `EntraIDFetchAuthArgs` coverage)
- [ ] Extended the documentation
- [ ] Provided sample for the feature

## Testing

### Reproduction

The bug was reproduced empirically against an Azure Managed Redis cluster with a workload-identity-bound managed identity that has the default access policy, using daprd 1.17.2:

1. Deploy a sidecar with a `state.redis` component configured as `useEntraID: true, redisType: cluster, enableTLS: true`.
2. Run a workload that exercises the state store directly via `DaprClient.GetState`/`SaveState` (Dapr Actors-only consumers don't surface this because actor state-store access is lazy).
3. Observe pods running cleanly for ~24 hours, then transitioning to `CrashLoopBackOff` with the daprd sidecar logging `WRONGPASS invalid username-password pair` from `state.redis` calls. The crossover aligns with the initial token's TTL.

I also verified the AUTH contract independently with a manual probe from inside the pod:

- Federated workload-identity → Entra exchange succeeds for scope `https://redis.azure.com/.default` and returns a token with the correct `aud` / `appid` / `oid`.
- Sending that token to AMR as `AUTH <token>` (no username) returns `WRONGPASS`.
- Sending the same token as `AUTH <oid> <token>` returns `+OK`.

This matches the documented AMR auth contract — confirms it's a client-side issue, not an authorization-policy issue.

### After this change

- Built a daprd image from `dapr/dapr` v1.17.2 with `go.mod` pointing at this branch via `replace`, deployed to the same cluster, configured the state-store with `maxConnAge: 5m` to force the pool to recycle every minute (rather than waiting for the natural ambient cadence).
- Pods came up cleanly. Both Redis components (`state.redis` and `pubsub.redis`) loaded successfully.
- Sidecar has been running across many full pool-cycle windows (every 5 min the entire pool is replaced; OnConnect fires per dial). **Zero `WRONGPASS` errors** across this period and zero sidecar restarts.
- Long-soak validation (>24h, past the original cliff) is in progress and will be reported back here.

### Edge cases considered

- v8/v9 cluster mode: each backend node returned by `CLUSTER SHARDS` gets the OnConnect hook applied, so dials to discovered IPs use a fresh token each time, not the original snapshot.
- Failover/sentinel: same — `FailoverOptions.OnConnect` is wired.
- The previously-static `Settings.Username` and `Settings.Password` are now empty when `useEntraID` is true. The pre-existing validation in `InitEntraIDCredential` (formerly `GetEntraIDCredentialAndSetInitialTokenAsPassword`) already errored when these were configured externally with `useEntraID`, so no behavior change for misconfiguration.
- Backward compat: components with explicit `redisUsername` / `redisPassword` and `useEntraID: false` are unaffected (the OnConnect hook is gated on `s.UseEntraID`).

### Also fixed here: `AuthACL` pipeline was never executed

While debugging, I noticed that `(c v8Client).AuthACL` and the v9 equivalent used `c.client.Pipeline()` to send the AUTH ACL command, but never called `Pipeline.Exec(ctx)`. In go-redis v8/v9 that means the queued AUTH command was never actually sent over the wire — the background refresh goroutine had been a silent no-op since #3632 landed, so long-lived idle connections never received the periodic re-AUTH the goroutine was meant to provide.

This is now fixed in this PR (both v8 and v9): the pipeline is `Exec`'d and its error propagated. Note that go-redis's `UniversalClient` only exposes `Cmdable` (not `StatefulCmdable`), so AUTH genuinely has to go through a pipeline here — calling `AuthACL` directly on the client doesn't compile; the missing piece was the `Exec` call.

### Other review follow-ups addressed

- `EntraIDFetchAuthArgs` now returns a clear error when the derived OID username is empty, instead of issuing `AUTH ACL "" <token>` and surfacing a confusing `WRONGPASS`.
- Added unit tests (see the checklist above).

## Refs

- #3554 (issue this PR closes)
- #3632 (the prior partial fix this PR completes)
- [Microsoft Learn: Use Microsoft Entra for cache authentication with Azure Managed Redis](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/redis/entra-for-authentication)

comments.body: `AuthACL` queues the command on a pipeline but never calls `Exec`, so the AUTH is never sent. This makes `StartEntraIDTokenRefreshBackgroundRoutine` ineffective (it can silently succeed while doing nothing). Call `AuthACL` directly on the client (or `Exec` the pipeline) so the command is actually executed.

`AuthACL` queues the command on a pipeline but never calls `Exec`, so the AUTH is never sent. This makes `StartEntraIDTokenRefreshBackgroundRoutine` ineffective (it can silently succeed while doing nothing). Call `AuthACL` directly on the client (or `Exec` the pipeline) so the command is actually executed.

`EntraIDFetchAuthArgs` returns `s.entraIDUsername` without validating it was initialized. If this is ever called before `InitEntraIDCredential` (or if parsing fails to set it in a future change), the code will attempt `AUTH ACL "" <token>` which fails with a confusing WRONGPASS. Return a clear error when the derived username (OID) is empty.

The new Entra ID `OnConnect` authentication path is central to fixing token TTL issues, but there are no unit tests asserting it is wired when `UseEntraID` is true (and not wired otherwise). Adding a focused test would help prevent regressions (e.g., options accidentally start setting `Password` again or `OnConnect` not being set for one client variant).

`AuthACL` queues the command on a pipeline but never calls `Exec`, so the AUTH is never sent. This makes `StartEntraIDTokenRefreshBackgroundRoutine` ineffective (it can silently succeed while doing nothing). Call `AuthACL` directly on the client (or `Exec` the pipeline) so the command is actually executed.

`AuthACL` queues the command on a pipeline but never calls `Exec`, so the AUTH is never sent. This makes `StartEntraIDTokenRefreshBackgroundRoutine` ineffective (it can silently succeed while doing nothing). Call `AuthACL` directly on the client (or `Exec` the pipeline) so the command is actually executed.

`EntraIDFetchAuthArgs` returns `s.entraIDUsername` without validating it was initialized. If this is ever called before `InitEntraIDCredential` (or if parsing fails to set it in a future change), the code will attempt `AUTH ACL "" <token>` which fails with a confusing WRONGPASS. Return a clear error when the derived username (OID) is empty.

The new Entra ID `OnConnect` authentication path is central to fixing token TTL issues, but there are no unit tests asserting it is wired when `UseEntraID` is true (and not wired otherwise). Adding a focused test would help prevent regressions (e.g., options accidentally start setting `Password` again or `OnConnect` not being set for one client variant).

Storing a pointer to an interface (`*azcore.TokenCredential`) is a Go anti-pattern — `azcore.TokenCredential` is already an interface and can hold any concrete implementation directly. The existing code in `redis.go` also passes `*azcore.TokenCredential` around, but for this new field there is no compatibility reason to do so. Consider storing it as `azcore.TokenCredential` directly, which removes the awkward `(*s.entraIDTokenCredential).GetToken(...)` dereference in `EntraIDFetchAuthArgs` and the `&cred` taking in `InitEntraIDCredential`.

Now that `entraIDUsername` and `entraIDTokenCredential` are stored on `Settings`, the `username`, `nextExpiration`, and `cred` arguments to `StartEntraIDTokenRefreshBackgroundRoutine` largely duplicate state already reachable via the Settings struct. Consider simplifying the signature to accept `*Settings` (or fetching the username from settings inside the goroutine) to avoid two sources of truth that can drift apart.

The tests verify that `OnConnect` is wired and `Password` is empty, but never actually invoke the `OnConnect` callback to confirm it produces a fresh token via `EntraIDFetchAuthArgs`. Since the bug being fixed is precisely that the wrong credential gets used at connect-time, consider adding a test that calls the captured `OnConnect`-equivalent path (e.g., directly invoking `entraIDOnConnectV8`/`entraIDOnConnectV9` returned function with a stub `*v8.Conn`/`*v9.Conn` or refactoring so the token-fetch logic is testable in isolation) to validate the fresh-token behavior end-to-end.

`OnConnect` is invoked synchronously on every new pool connection and now performs a token acquisition. While `azcore` credentials cache tokens, the first connection after a refresh window — or any transient credential error — will block (or fail) the dial. Consider: (1) ensuring the context passed in by go-redis has a sensible deadline, and (2) confirming the failure behavior (a returned error here closes the connection and propagates to the caller of the Redis command, which is the desired behavior but is worth being explicit about in the doc comment). Same applies to `entraIDOnConnectV8`.

The pipeline created by `c.client.Pipeline()` should be closed to avoid leaking resources across repeated calls. Consider adding `defer pipeline.Close()` (or using `Pipelined(...)`) so any underlying resources are released deterministically after `Exec`.
Same as v8: the pipeline should be closed after use. Add `defer pipeline.Close()` (or switch to `Pipelined(...)`) to ensure resources are released even when `Exec` returns early.

If the `oid` claim is present but empty, init will succeed and later calls will fail at runtime (e.g., `EntraIDFetchAuthArgs` rejects empty usernames). Treat an empty `objectID` as a configuration error here and return early, so misconfigured/invalid tokens fail fast during initialization.

These helpers assume the concrete `RedisClient` implementation stored in the interface is `v8Client`/`v9Client` by value. If the constructors return `*v8Client`/`*v9Client` (common even when methods have value receivers), the type assertions will panic and the tests will fail. Make the helpers handle both pointer and value forms (e.g., via a type switch on `c` for `v8Client` and `*v8Client`, and similarly for v9) before accessing the embedded `.client`.

These helpers assume the concrete `RedisClient` implementation stored in the interface is `v8Client`/`v9Client` by value. If the constructors return `*v8Client`/`*v9Client` (common even when methods have value receivers), the type assertions will panic and the tests will fail. Make the helpers handle both pointer and value forms (e.g., via a type switch on `c` for `v8Client` and `*v8Client`, and similarly for v9) before accessing the embedded `.client`.

Good call — fixed in daa94608. `InitEntraIDCredential` now returns a config error when the `oid` claim is present but empty, so a misconfigured token fails fast at init rather than as a confusing connect-time `WRONGPASS`.

This one doesn't apply: go-redis's `Pipeliner` interface has no `Close()` method (only `Do`/`Process`/`Discard`/`Exec`/`Len`), so `defer pipeline.Close()` would not compile. A plain (non-Tx) pipeline also doesn't hold a dedicated connection — it borrows one from the pool only for the duration of `Exec` and returns it immediately — so there's nothing to leak here.

Same as the v8 case: `Pipeliner` has no `Close()` method, so this wouldn't compile, and a non-Tx pipeline only borrows a pooled connection during `Exec` (returning it right after), so there's nothing to release.

These are safe as-is: `newV8Client`/`newV9Client` and their failover variants all `return v8Client{...}` / `v9Client{...}` by **value** (the methods use value receivers), so the `c.(v8Client)` / `c.(v9Client)` assertions match the stored concrete type and won't panic. The tests pass in CI for exactly this reason. I'm happy to add pointer cases to the type switch defensively if you'd prefer, but there's no current code path that returns a pointer.
Same as the v8 helper above — the constructors return `v8Client`/`v9Client` by value, so the value-form type assertion is correct and matches what's stored in the interface.

The pipeline is created but never closed. In go-redis, it’s safer to either `defer pipeline.Close()` after creation or use `Pipelined(ctx, func(pipe ...) error { ... })` to ensure pipeline resources/state are released promptly (especially if this method is called repeatedly).
Same as v9: the pipeline is never closed. Consider `defer pipeline.Close()` or switching to `Pipelined` so the pipeline is reliably cleaned up after `Exec`.

The refresh routine launches an unbounded goroutine with a cancelable context, but there is no external path to trigger `cancel()`. This can leak goroutines if components are reloaded or clients are recreated. Consider accepting a parent `context.Context` (or a stop channel) from the caller and deriving `ctx` from it, so shutdown (or client Close) can terminate the refresh loop cleanly.

`EntraIDFetchAuthArgs` is newly exported, which expands the package API surface. If this helper is intended for internal wiring only (OnConnect + refresh routine), consider making it unexported (e.g., `entraIDFetchAuthArgs`) and keeping tests in the same `redis` package to avoid exposing it publicly.

`EntraIDFetchAuthArgs` is exported and its doc states it must only be called when `UseEntraID` is true, but the implementation does not enforce that contract. To prevent accidental misuse, either (a) add an explicit `if !s.UseEntraID { ... }` guard returning an error, or (b) make the method unexported since it appears intended for internal wiring (OnConnect/refresh) only.

This comment overstates what the refresh goroutine can guarantee: `AUTH` is connection-specific, and calling `client.AuthACL(...)` on a pooled client typically authenticates only the single connection chosen for that command (not all existing pooled connections). Suggest rewording to reflect the actual behavior (e.g., refreshes authentication on connections as they are exercised / opportunistically) to avoid misleading operators/debugging.

The comment says \"Validate the initial token\" but the code explicitly disables verification and validation (`WithVerify(false)`, `WithValidate(false)`). Please adjust the wording to avoid implying cryptographic/claims validation is performed here (e.g., \"Parse the initial token without verification to extract the OID claim\").

This fixes a critical behavioral bug (AUTH previously never sent because the pipeline wasn't Exec'd). Please add a unit test that would have failed before this change by asserting `AuthACL` results in `Exec` being called (e.g., by using a lightweight fake `UniversalClient`/`Pipeliner` that records whether `Exec` ran, or an existing redis mock if the repo already uses one). This should cover both v8 and v9 implementations (can share the fake).

The callback error-path tests cover missing-OID for v8 but not v9. Add the matching v9 case (`entraIDOnConnectV9`) to keep parity and ensure both callbacks consistently surface missing OID as a dial error.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,202 @@
+/*
+Copyright 2021 The Dapr Authors
+Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
+you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
+You may obtain a copy of the License at
+    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
+Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
+distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
+WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
+See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
+limitations under the License.
+*/
+
+package redis
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"errors"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azcore"
+	"github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azcore/policy"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
+
+	v8 "github.com/go-redis/redis/v8"
+	v9 "github.com/redis/go-redis/v9"
+)
+
+// fakeTokenCredential is a stub azcore.TokenCredential for exercising the
+// Entra ID auth path without contacting Azure.
+type fakeTokenCredential struct {
+	token string
+	err   error
+}
+
+func (f fakeTokenCredential) GetToken(context.Context, policy.TokenRequestOptions) (azcore.AccessToken, error) {
+	if f.err != nil {
+		return azcore.AccessToken{}, f.err
+	}
+	return azcore.AccessToken{Token: f.token, ExpiresOn: time.Now().Add(time.Hour)}, nil
+}
+
+// entraIDSettings returns Settings wired as InitEntraIDCredential would leave
+// them after a successful init, but with a stubbed credential.
+func entraIDSettings(redisType string, failover bool) *Settings {
+	return &Settings{
+		Host:                   "localhost:6379",
+		RedisType:              redisType,
+		Failover:               failover,
+		UseEntraID:             true,
+		entraIDUsername:        "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
+		entraIDTokenCredential: fakeTokenCredential{token: "fresh-token"},
+	}
+}
+
+// v8OnConnectAndPassword extracts the OnConnect callback and Password configured
+// on the underlying go-redis v8 client, across the node/cluster variants.
+func v8OnConnectAndPassword(t *testing.T, c RedisClient) (onConnect any, password string) {
+	t.Helper()
+	switch client := c.(v8Client).client.(type) {
+	case *v8.Client:
+		return client.Options().OnConnect, client.Options().Password
+	case *v8.ClusterClient:
+		return client.Options().OnConnect, client.Options().Password
+	default:
+		t.Fatalf("unexpected v8 client type %T", client)
+		return nil, ""
+	}
+}
+
+// v9OnConnectAndPassword extracts the OnConnect callback and Password configured
+// on the underlying go-redis v9 client, across the node/cluster variants.
+func v9OnConnectAndPassword(t *testing.T, c RedisClient) (onConnect any, password string) {
+	t.Helper()
+	switch client := c.(v9Client).client.(type) {
+	case *v9.Client:
+		return client.Options().OnConnect, client.Options().Password
+	case *v9.ClusterClient:
+		return client.Options().OnConnect, client.Options().Password
+	default:
+		t.Fatalf("unexpected v9 client type %T", client)
+		return nil, ""
+	}
+}
+
+func TestEntraIDOnConnectWiring(t *testing.T) {
+	cases := []struct {
+		name      string
+		redisType string
+		failover  bool
+		newV8     func(*Settings) (RedisClient, error)
+		newV9     func(*Settings) (RedisClient, error)
+	}{
+		{name: "node", redisType: NodeType, newV8: newV8Client, newV9: newV9Client},
+		{name: "cluster", redisType: ClusterType, newV8: newV8Client, newV9: newV9Client},
+		{name: "failover", redisType: NodeType, failover: true, newV8: newV8FailoverClient, newV9: newV9FailoverClient},
+		{name: "failover-cluster", redisType: ClusterType, failover: true, newV8: newV8FailoverClient, newV9: newV9FailoverClient},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range cases {
+		t.Run("v8/"+tc.name+"/entraID-enabled", func(t *testing.T) {
+			c, err := tc.newV8(entraIDSettings(tc.redisType, tc.failover))
+			require.NoError(t, err)
+			require.NotNil(t, c)
+			defer c.Close()
+
+			onConnect, password := v8OnConnectAndPassword(t, c)
+			assert.NotNil(t, onConnect, "OnConnect must be wired when UseEntraID is true")
+			assert.Empty(t, password, "static Password must not be set when UseEntraID is true")
+		})
+
+		t.Run("v8/"+tc.name+"/entraID-disabled", func(t *testing.T) {
+			s := entraIDSettings(tc.redisType, tc.failover)
+			s.UseEntraID = false
+			c, err := tc.newV8(s)
+			require.NoError(t, err)
+			require.NotNil(t, c)
+			defer c.Close()
+
+			onConnect, _ := v8OnConnectAndPassword(t, c)
+			assert.Nil(t, onConnect, "OnConnect must not be wired when UseEntraID is false")
+		})
+
+		t.Run("v9/"+tc.name+"/entraID-enabled", func(t *testing.T) {
+			c, err := tc.newV9(entraIDSettings(tc.redisType, tc.failover))
+			require.NoError(t, err)
+			require.NotNil(t, c)
+			defer c.Close()
+
+			onConnect, password := v9OnConnectAndPassword(t, c)
+			assert.NotNil(t, onConnect, "OnConnect must be wired when UseEntraID is true")
+			assert.Empty(t, password, "static Password must not be set when UseEntraID is true")
+		})
+
+		t.Run("v9/"+tc.name+"/entraID-disabled", func(t *testing.T) {
+			s := entraIDSettings(tc.redisType, tc.failover)
+			s.UseEntraID = false
+			c, err := tc.newV9(s)
+			require.NoError(t, err)
+			require.NotNil(t, c)
+			defer c.Close()
+
+			onConnect, _ := v9OnConnectAndPassword(t, c)
+			assert.Nil(t, onConnect, "OnConnect must not be wired when UseEntraID is false")
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestEntraIDFetchAuthArgs(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("returns username and fresh token", func(t *testing.T) {
+		s := entraIDSettings(NodeType, false)
+		user, pass, err := s.EntraIDFetchAuthArgs(context.Background())
+		require.NoError(t, err)
+		assert.Equal(t, "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000", user)
+		assert.Equal(t, "fresh-token", pass)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("errors when credential not initialized", func(t *testing.T) {
+		s := &Settings{UseEntraID: true, entraIDUsername: "oid"}
+		_, _, err := s.EntraIDFetchAuthArgs(context.Background())
+		require.Error(t, err)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("errors when username (OID) not initialized", func(t *testing.T) {
+		s := &Settings{UseEntraID: true, entraIDTokenCredential: fakeTokenCredential{token: "fresh-token"}}
+		_, _, err := s.EntraIDFetchAuthArgs(context.Background())
+		require.Error(t, err)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("propagates token acquisition error", func(t *testing.T) {
+		s := &Settings{UseEntraID: true, entraIDUsername: "oid", entraIDTokenCredential: fakeTokenCredential{err: errors.New("boom")}}
+		_, _, err := s.EntraIDFetchAuthArgs(context.Background())
+		require.Error(t, err)
+	})
+}
+
+// TestEntraIDOnConnectCallback invokes the OnConnect callbacks themselves (not just
+// asserting they are wired) to confirm they drive authentication through a fresh-token
+// fetch and surface a credential failure as a dial error. A failing credential makes the
+// callback return before it touches the (nil) connection, so this exercises the
+// connect-time auth path end-to-end without a live Redis server.
+func TestEntraIDOnConnectCallback(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("v8 surfaces token-fetch failure as a dial error", func(t *testing.T) {
+		s := &Settings{UseEntraID: true, entraIDUsername: "oid", entraIDTokenCredential: fakeTokenCredential{err: errors.New("boom")}}
+		err := entraIDOnConnectV8(s)(context.Background(), nil)
+		require.Error(t, err)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("v9 surfaces token-fetch failure as a dial error", func(t *testing.T) {
+		s := &Settings{UseEntraID: true, entraIDUsername: "oid", entraIDTokenCredential: fakeTokenCredential{err: errors.New("boom")}}
+		err := entraIDOnConnectV9(s)(context.Background(), nil)
+		require.Error(t, err)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("v8 surfaces missing-OID as a dial error", func(t *testing.T) {
+		s := &Settings{UseEntraID: true, entraIDTokenCredential: fakeTokenCredential{token: "fresh-token"}}
+		err := entraIDOnConnectV8(s)(context.Background(), nil)
+		require.Error(t, err)
+	})
+}

@@ -14,18 +14,28 @@ limitations under the License.
 package redis
 
 import (
+	"context"
 	"crypto/tls"
+	"errors"
 	"fmt"
 	"net"
 	"strconv"
 	"strings"
 	"time"
 
+	"github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azcore"
+	"github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azcore/policy"
+
 	"github.com/dapr/kit/config"
 )
 
 const defaultRedisPort = "6379"
 
+// entraIDRedisScope is the OAuth scope used to acquire Entra ID access tokens for
+// Azure Cache/Managed Redis. Kept as a single constant so the initial-token fetch,
+// the per-connection OnConnect fetch, and the background refresh loop never drift.
+const entraIDRedisScope = "https://redis.azure.com/.default"
+
 type Settings struct {
 	// The Redis host
 	Host string `mapstructure:"redisHost"`
@@ -123,6 +133,48 @@ type Settings struct {
 	// EntraID / AzureAD Authentication based on the shared code which essentially uses the DefaultAzureCredential
 	// from the official Azure Identity SDK for Go
 	UseEntraID bool `mapstructure:"useEntraID" mapstructurealiases:"useAzureAD"`
